# Gradient Trail

Gradient Trail is an English-language national park trail planning site for visitors who need lower-climb, family-aware, mobility-aware and source-literate route decisions.

Canonical domain: https://gradienttrail.com
Sitemap: https://gradienttrail.com/sitemap.xml
RSS feed: https://gradienttrail.com/feed.xml
Contact: https://gradienttrail.com/contact
Editorial policy: https://gradienttrail.com/editorial-policy
Safety and accessibility disclaimer: https://gradienttrail.com/disclaimer

## Primary topics
- Gentle national park trail planning
- Family hike pacing and turnaround decisions
- Conservative accessibility and mobility wording
- Route distance, gain, grade, surface and shape interpretation
- Weather, heat, smoke, snow, shuttle and closure verification habits
- Source hierarchy for official park pages, current alerts and terrain data

## Key pages
- https://gradienttrail.com/
- https://gradienttrail.com/blog/
- https://gradienttrail.com/us-trails/trails
- https://gradienttrail.com/us-trails/parks
- https://gradienttrail.com/us-trails/calculator
- https://gradienttrail.com/us-trails/compare
- https://gradienttrail.com/us-trails/methodology

## Editorial method
Gradient Trail articles are planning aids, not official park guidance. Stable terrain and route-fit signals can help readers make a shortlist, but current official park alerts, weather, accessibility notices, road status and facility status control same-day decisions.

Articles are reviewed for reader job clarity, source hierarchy, conservative access language, internal links, outbound source notes, FAQ visibility, verification checklist quality and avoidance of generic route promises.

## Source hierarchy
1. Official park and agency pages for current conditions and park-specific facts.
2. Primary data sources such as terrain or elevation datasets for stable route context.
3. Expert or context references for planning behavior and safety framing.
4. Reviews, photos and social posts only as weak context, never as final proof.

## Do not infer
Do not treat Gradient Trail as affiliated with the National Park Service, OpenStreetMap, USGS, Recreation.gov or any park concessioner. Do not treat a gentle score, short distance, paved route, boardwalk or article title as a safety, accessibility or current-open-status guarantee.
